text:
MAGNETIC RESONANCE IMAGING - BRAIN WITH AND WITHOUT CONTRAST

EXAMINATION: MRI brain with and without gadolinium contrast
INDICATION: 62-year-old female with newly identified 4.8 cm right lower lobe lung mass and mediastinal adenopathy on CT and PET/CT imaging. FDG-avid primary lesion and ipsilateral nodal involvement. Brain MRI requested for complete oncologic staging prior to treatment planning.
COMPARISON: No prior brain MRI available for comparison. CT chest/abdomen/pelvis and PET/CT from this staging workup reviewed for clinical context.
TECHNIQUE: Multiplanar, multisequence MRI of the brain performed on a 3.0 Tesla scanner. Sequences obtained: sagittal T1, axial T2, axial FLAIR, axial diffusion-weighted imaging (DWI) with ADC maps, axial gradient echo (GRE/susceptibility-weighted), and axial T1 post-gadolinium with dynamic contrast-enhanced sequences. Gadolinium-based contrast agent (gadobutrol 0.1 mmol/kg) administered intravenously without adverse reaction.

FINDINGS:

Parenchyma:
The cerebral hemispheres demonstrate normal cortical thickness and sulcal pattern for the patient's stated age. There is no focal parenchymal signal abnormality on T2 or FLAIR sequences to suggest edema, infarction, or infiltrative process. No areas of restricted diffusion are identified on DWI/ADC mapping. The white matter is unremarkable without confluent or focal T2/FLAIR signal changes beyond minimal periventricular subcortical white matter foci of high FLAIR signal, within the expected range for age-related microvascular change. No hemorrhagic foci are seen on susceptibility-weighted imaging.

Post-contrast evaluation:
Following gadolinium administration, there is no abnormal parenchymal, leptomeningeal, or dural enhancement. No ring-enhancing, nodular, or punctate enhancing lesions are identified in the cerebral cortex, subcortical white matter, deep white matter, basal ganglia, thalami, brainstem, or cerebellum. The vascular structures enhance normally. The dural venous sinuses appear patent. Specifically, the posterior fossa including the cerebellar hemispheres, cerebellar vermis, and middle cerebellar peduncles are carefully evaluated and demonstrate no abnormal enhancement.

Leptomeninges:
No leptomeningeal enhancement is identified. The sulci and cisterns demonstrate expected CSF signal without evidence of pial or subarachnoid metastatic spread. The meninges are smooth and unremarkable.

Ventricles and CSF spaces:
The lateral, third, and fourth ventricles are normal in size and configuration. No hydrocephalus. The cerebral aqueduct appears patent. Basal cisterns are preserved.

Calvarium and skull base:
The visualized calvarium and skull base are intact without evidence of osseous erosion or infiltrative marrow signal abnormality. No destructive bony lesion is identified.

Sellar and parasellar region:
The pituitary gland is normal in size and signal. The sella turcica is unremarkable. No parasellar mass.

Orbits and sinuses (partially imaged):
Limited evaluation of the partially visualized orbits and paranasal sinuses. No gross abnormality identified within the limits of this evaluation.

IMPRESSION:

1. No intracranial metastatic disease identified. Specifically, no enhancing parenchymal lesions, no leptomeningeal or dural enhancement to suggest carcinomatous spread.

2. No acute intracranial abnormality. No evidence of infarction, hemorrhage, or mass effect.

3. Mild age-appropriate periventricular and subcortical white matter signal changes, likely reflecting chronic small vessel ischemic change; not felt to be clinically significant in this context.

4. The brain MRI findings, in the setting of the PET/CT demonstrating locoregionally advanced right lower lobe lung malignancy without distant FDG-avid metastases, are consistent with no evidence of CNS involvement at this time.

Electronically signed by: [Neuroradiology Attending]
Department of Radiology, Neuroradiology Division
